text.title: BA in Scaled Agile (SAFe)
text.summary: SAFe level-to-BA-role mapping, key activities per level, and relevant certifications with market data.

rule.statement: Map BA responsibilities to SAFe levels: Team Level (BA acts as Product Owner — user stories, acceptance criteria, backlog management), Program Level (Senior BA / System Architect — domain modeling, feature analysis, cross-team dependency analysis), Large Solution Level (Solution Analyst — cross-ART coordination, capability definition), Portfolio Level (Enterprise BA — portfolio backlog grooming, strategic alignment, business case analysis).
rule.rationale: SAFe does not define a BA role explicitly; without an explicit mapping, BA competencies are either lost (BA has no role) or duplicated (BA and PO both own the same artifacts).

list.item: Bridge business and IT: translate business needs to technical requirements at all levels
Facilitate communication: enable collaboration among stakeholders across ARTs and teams
Prioritize by value: support value-based feature prioritization using WSJF or RICE at program level
Define vision and roadmap: support Product Management with strategic direction and capability definition
Ensure alignment: verify agile delivery aligns with business goals across the program increment
Agile Analysis Certification (AAC) — IIBA: 71% of BAs practice agile; AAC holders report 16% salary premium over non-certified
SAFe Agilist (SA) — Scaled Agile: enterprise agile scaling, PI Planning facilitation
SAFe Product Owner / Product Manager (POPM) — Scaled Agile: backlog management at Team and Program levels
Certified Product Ownership Analysis (CPOA) — IIBA: product ownership analysis skills for BA-to-PO transition
